title: Step 4c silent-skip recurrence after PROP-072 hoist: fix mis-titled commit (4c's commit grabs Step 3 universal-pusher rescues under wrong title when 4c writes zero sentinels) + replace mtime-based SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE backstop with filename-ISO parse + instrument script with absolute write path
author: tinker
source: Mode 3 follow-up from tinker-2026-05-31T17-51 forward-watch; previous run set the trigger 'if 18:00 also fails, author PROP-073'. Three consecutive post-PROP-072 cycles (17:22, 18:10, 19:22) all failed to produce a sync-workspace-runs sentinel in git.
executive_summary: PROP-072 hoisted the Step 4c helper invocation to FIRST position in workspace-sync's collapsed Step 4 block to defeat the LLM mid-block skip pattern. Empirically the hoist did NOT fix the silent-skip — three consecutive cycles (17:22, 18:10, 19:22) emit commits titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: <ts>' but each commit's tree contains ZERO sync-workspace-runs-*.json / sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-*.json / sync-workspace-step4c-crash-*.json files. Most recent actual sentinel file remains 2026-05-31T09-07-58Z, ~11h stale. Diagnosis: (a) the node helper script is NOT being invoked (no sentinel ever lands on disk in the clone), AND (b) Step 4c's `git add <sentinel-globs> 2>/dev/null || true` matches zero files but the subsequent `git diff --cached --quiet` evaluates the index left dirty by Step 3 (smart_copy + universal-pusher), so the commit fires under the WRONG title with WRONG contents (analyst rescues mis-attributed as step4c sentinels), AND (c) the secondary-fix `SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE` mtime-based heuristic that PROP-072 added to AGENT_NOTES guidance is broken because `git clone` resets all sentinel file mtimes to clone time, producing false-positive counts of 3-4 even when zero sentinels were written this cycle. PROP-073 proposes three structural fixes: (1) make 4c's commit STAGE-DELTA-GATED so it only fires if the `git add` actually added new sentinel files (defeats mis-titled-commit failure mode), (2) replace `SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE` with a filename-ISO-timestamp parse that ignores mtime (defeats backstop false-positive), (3) instrument `sync-workspace-step4c.js` to `console.log` the absolute path it wrote to (gives ground-truth signal when LLM does invoke the script, distinguishing 'invoked + wrong cwd' from 'never invoked').

post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.run_report_agent_notes: tracker-archive.jsonl FUSE-newer divergence logged (producer-bug canary). step4c ran (SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE=4).
post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.comment: Agent BELIEVED step4c ran because SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE=4. But the commit grabbed Step 3 analyst rescues, not 4c sentinels. Confirms both bugs co-occur.

post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.run_report_agent_notes: Routine sync: 51 skips (all mtime-guard or git-append-only; no strands). Deploy clean.
post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.comment: Self-attestation backstop NOT emitted (no 'step4c-not-invoked' in AGENT_NOTES). Agent unaware Step 4c silently skipped.

post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.run_report_agent_notes: Synced EXP-489/ISS-2317 analyst outputs + status.json + analysis-summary + skips-log.
post_prop_072_workspace_sync_cycles.comment: Same pattern. Three cycles. Self-attestation backstop silent.

observed_evidence.staleness_at_authoring_time: ~11 hours since last actual Step 4c sentinel landed in git
observed_evidence.manual_invocation_test_at_authoring_time: Tinker confirmed script is verbatim-present at monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js (11111 bytes), unchanged from PROP-066 deploy. Script logic verified — its writeSentinel function always runs through one of three branches (success / non-ff abort / crash), every branch ends with a fs.writeFileSync to monitor/integrity/. So 'script invoked + ran but wrote nothing' is not plausible — the only branches that produce zero on-disk artifact are (i) crash before reaching uncaughtException handler, which itself writes a crash sentinel via writeSentinel, OR (ii) the script was never invoked at all.

root_cause_analysis.primary_root_cause: LLM workspace-sync agent is STILL not actually invoking 'node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js', even after PROP-072 hoisted it to FIRST position in the collapsed Step 4 block. PROP-072's premise (LLM reliably runs FIRST and LAST positions in a bash block) is empirically refuted by three consecutive cycles.
root_cause_analysis.secondary_amplification_1_mis_titled_com: When Step 4c's node invocation is skipped, the subsequent 'git add monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json ...' matches zero files ('|| true' swallows the no-match). The 'git diff --cached --quiet' check then evaluates the index AS LEFT BY STEP 3 (smart_copy + universal-pusher pre-staged analyst/integrity/decisions files). The 'if' body fires under the WRONG TITLE ('PROP-066 step4c sentinel: <ts>') with WRONG CONTENTS (Step 3's universal-pusher files). This means: (a) the audit signal 'commit titled PROP-066 step4c sentinel == proof step4c ran' is FALSE-POSITIVE; (b) the Step 4a commit subsequently runs but finds the index empty, so Step 4a's 'Workspace sync: <ts>' commit either doesn't exist or contains zero files; (c) Step 4b's run-report becomes the only narrative artifact.
root_cause_analysis.secondary_amplification_2_mtime_heuristi: The PROP-072 SECONDARY backstop relies on SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE, computed via stat-mtime of pre-existing sentinel files. Because workspace-sync runs in a FRESH CLONE each cycle, all pre-existing sentinel files inherit clone-time mtimes (within the last few seconds). The mtime filter '($1 > now - 600)' matches ALL of them, producing count=3 or 4. The AGENT_NOTES guidance reads 'check $SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE captured above: it should be >=1 on every successful cycle' — the false-positive count >=1 satisfies the check, so the agent BELIEVES Step 4c ran and does NOT emit 'step4c-not-invoked' in AGENT_NOTES. The 17:22 run-report's 'step4c ran (SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE=4)' note is the smoking gun: agent reported success based on heuristic-the-clone-broke.

problem.why_prop_072_was_necessary_but_not_suffi: PROP-072's hypothesis (FIRST-position skip-resistance) is partially correct in that the LLM DOES execute the FIRST bash line of the block (the trap). But it does NOT reliably execute the second line ('node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js'). The hypothesis 'FIRST and LAST positions are skip-resistant' is too weak — what the LLM appears to reliably execute is the OPENING SETUP (trap) and the SUBSEQUENT CONTENT-PRODUCING ACTIONS (commit + push). A pure-side-effect node invocation that produces no shell-stdout-relevant variable falls in the same skip-zone as middle-of-block actions. The cure: (a) prevent the misleading false-positive signal (mis-titled commit + broken mtime heuristic), (b) make non-invocation IMMEDIATELY visible in the run-report, (c) provide an independent invocation proof channel (script logs its own write path).

primary_fix_stage_delta_gating.location: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md, Step 4 collapsed bash block, the 4c sub-section
primary_fix_stage_delta_gating.current_pattern_summary: git add <sentinel-globs> 2>/dev/null || true; if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then git commit -m 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel'; git push; fi
primary_fix_stage_delta_gating.proposed_pattern_summary: PRE_ADD_STAGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only | wc -l); git add <sentinel-globs>; POST_ADD_STAGED=$(git diff --cached --name-only | wc -l); ADDED_BY_4C=$((POST_ADD_STAGED - PRE_ADD_STAGED)); if [ $ADDED_BY_4C -gt 0 ]; then git commit ...; git push; else echo '[PROP-073] step4c added zero sentinels; deferring pre-staged files to Step 4a'; fi
primary_fix_stage_delta_gating.correctness: Conservative-and-safe. If Step 4c actually wrote a sentinel, ADDED_BY_4C > 0 and the commit fires correctly. If skipped, pre-staged files flow through to Step 4a's commit with the correct 'Workspace sync: <ts>' title. The audit signal 'sync-workspace-runs sentinel commit absent => Step 4c skipped' becomes reliable.
primary_fix_stage_delta_gating.rollback_safe: Pure additive change; if it misfires, Step 4a's commit still runs unchanged.

secondary_fix_filename_iso_parse.proposed_pattern_summary: ls -1 <sentinel-globs> | awk 'match($0, /[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}Z/) { iso=substr(...); gsub-normalize; pipe to date -u -d <iso> +%s; if (ts > now-600) c++ }'
secondary_fix_filename_iso_parse.correctness: Filename-ISO parse uses the in-name timestamp set by the script at write time; independent of filesystem mtime. A fresh clone seeing filenames like '2026-05-31T09-07-58Z' (11h old by name) correctly excludes them. Pre-existing sentinels never produce false positives.
secondary_fix_filename_iso_parse.fallback: If 'date -u -d' is unavailable on the runtime, the pattern still does not false-positive — it simply produces 0 (correct when no current-cycle sentinel was written). False-positives are the failure mode this change closes.

tertiary_fix_script_logs_absolute_write_.correctness: Purely diagnostic — no behavior change. path.resolve(p) gives an absolute path which is unambiguous about which working tree the script wrote to. If workspace-sync ever invokes the script with wrong cwd (e.g., FUSE instead of clone), the log line will reveal it.
tertiary_fix_script_logs_absolute_write_.audit_use: Tinker's next Mode 1 audit greps run-report bash output for '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel: /tmp/<clone-dir>/monitor/integrity/...' and treats absence as primary skip evidence.

quaternary_fix_agent_notes_guidance_upda.change: Update the 'step4c-not-invoked' bullet to reference the FILENAME-ISO-parsed SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE (from secondary fix). Add a new bullet: 'step4c-mis-titled' — if a commit titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: ...' exists in this cycle's git log but no sync-workspace-runs-*.json file was added in that commit, the stage-delta gate failed open and Step 3's pre-staged files were mis-attributed. Include 'step4c-mis-titled' in AGENT_NOTES.
quaternary_fix_agent_notes_guidance_upda.rationale: Adds a second observable failure-mode signal so a future regression (if PROP-073's stage-delta gate were ever reverted or weakened) becomes immediately visible without a full PROP cycle.

alternatives_considered.alt: Defer Step 4c to a separate scheduled task (new agent dome-git-to-fuse-sync)
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Adds an agent, cron entry, PAT preamble, sentinel format. PROP-073 is minimal-cost if it fixes the invocation defect. Stand by as PROP-074 if PROP-073 also fails to land step4c sentinels reliably within 1 day of deploy.

alternatives_considered.alt: Convert Step 4c into an inline shell-only check that exits the bash block on detection of skip
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Step 4c is best-effort by design (PROP-066: 'caller does NOT propagate exit code'). Hard-exit on Step 4c skip would break Step 4a (FUSE→git rescue) which is more critical to pipeline health. Stage-delta gating gives same observability without breaking Step 4a.

alternatives_considered.alt: Self-apply this PROP from the tinker run authoring it
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Tinker's 'Conservative self-fixes' rule applies. PROP-073's three sub-fixes touch prompt logic and helper script logic. The previous tinker (17:51) authored PROP-072 and operator deployed it via DIRECTIVE-20260531-012 — established pattern. PROP-073 should follow the same path.

alternatives_considered.alt: Add a fail-loud assert at end of Step 4 that aborts if no step4c sentinel was committed this cycle
alternatives_considered.rejected_reason: Same reason as PROP-072 alternative-1: silent-skip-to-fail-loud-abort creates a new failure mode where genuine no-op cycles abort. Stage-delta gating captures the same signal without changing failure modes.

proposed_implementation.estimated_diff_size_lines: ~25 lines changed across two files, ~10 net new lines
proposed_implementation.deployment_via_directive: Suggested operator-directive title: DIRECTIVE-20260531-XXX — implement PROP-073 (Step 4c mis-titled commit + mtime backstop fix + script write-path instrumentation). Pattern mirrors DIRECTIVE-20260531-012 for PROP-072.
proposed_implementation.deployment_quick_check: After deploy, the next workspace-sync cycle should EITHER produce a new sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json sentinel committed under 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: <ts>' title (Step 4c actually invoked), OR produce a Step 4a commit titled 'Workspace sync: <ts>' containing Step 3's pre-staged files with NO 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' commit (Step 4c skipped but stage-delta-gate correctly suppressed the mis-titled commit). Both outcomes are observable and audit-distinguishable.

verification_pattern: grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'ADDED_BY_4C' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md && grep -q 'PROP-073' monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN

verification_path.synthetic_test_pre_deploy: In a fresh clone, manually run 'node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js'. Confirm '[PROP-066] wrote sentinel: /tmp/<clone>/monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json' appears with absolute path. Run the modified shell pattern: (a) when a sentinel was written, ADDED_BY_4C > 0 and the commit body would contain only sentinel files; (b) when no sentinel was written, ADDED_BY_4C = 0 and the commit is skipped.
verification_path.live_canary_post_deploy_1_cycle: Within ~1 hour of deploy, next workspace-sync cycle either: (a) commits a sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json file under 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' title (healthy), OR (b) commits ONLY 'Workspace sync: <ts>' with Step 3 pre-staged files (PROP-073 suppressed the mis-titled commit but underlying Step 4c LLM-skip persists; escalate to PROP-074).
verification_path.live_canary_post_deploy_3_cycles: After 3 hourly cycles: if ANY produced an actual sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json, invocation rate is non-zero. If ZERO across 3 cycles, LLM has fully detached from invoking the script — escalate to PROP-074.
verification_path.rollback_criterion: If post-deploy the gating misfires and Step 4a's commit also stops working, revert PROP-073. Would be obvious within one cycle. Pre-deploy synthetic test should catch this; backup rollback is git revert of the deploy commit.

non_goals: Replacing the dome-workspace-sync agent with a non-LLM cron job (deferred to PROP-074 if PROP-073 also fails)
Reverting PROP-072's FIRST-position hoist (that fix remains net-correct; PROP-073 is additive)
Refactoring sync-workspace-step4c.js's classification logic (script logic is verified; the defect is INVOCATION + COMMIT-ATTRIBUTION, not script behavior)
Adding a global workspace-sync run-failure counter or alerting (separate concern; defer to a future tinker-cost-engineering audit)
related_props: PROP-074 (placeholder — defer Step 4c to its own scheduled agent if PROP-073 also fails)

evidence_files: git show c9e1d92 (17:22 mis-titled commit, 0 step4c sentinels)
git show 007d0fe (18:10 mis-titled commit, 0 step4c sentinels)
git show b7c36cb (19:22 mis-titled commit, 0 step4c sentinels)
context_pointers.previous_tinker_handoff: tinker-2026-05-31T17-51 wrote: 'If 18:00 also fails → author PROP-073 with three sub-fixes: (a) replace SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE mtime heuristic with filename-ISO-timestamp parse (b) add a 'did git-add actually stage anything' branch — fail-loud if zero sentinels staged (c) instrument sync-workspace-step4c.js to log the absolute write path it used'. PROP-073 implements all three sub-fixes plus a quaternary AGENT_NOTES guidance update.

operator_action_requested: Review PROP-073 and (if approved) issue an operator-directive to a future tinker / claude session to implement the three patches in a clone, verify with the pre-deploy synthetic test, push to main, and watch the next 3 workspace-sync cycles for sentinel restoration. If 3 cycles fail post-deploy, escalate to PROP-074 (separate scheduled agent for git→FUSE propagation).
